title: Fermented Juicy Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A slow-cooked stew originating from Algeria, made with lamb, fermented vegetables, and a hint of clear honey, perfect for a cozy sick-day meal.

ingredients.name: 1 1/2 pounds boneless lamb shoulder, cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: You can use lamb shanks instead for a more tender meat.

ingredients.name: 1 large onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion variety like Vidalia.

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Adjust to your desired garlic intensity.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped carrots
ingredients.notes: You can use a food processor to speed up the chopping process.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped zucchini
ingredients.notes: Use a variety with a tender skin, like Courgette.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: You can use a sweet bell pepper variety.

ingredients.name: 1 cup fermented mixed vegetables (cucumbers, beets, cabbage)
ingredients.notes: Use a store-bought or homemade fermented mix.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, extra-virgin olive oil.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon clear honey
ingredients.notes: Adjust to your desired level of sweetness.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cumin for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground coriander
ingredients.notes: Use fresh coriander for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, fresh cinnamon stick for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: Adjust to your desired level of spiciness.

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb and cook, stirring occasionally, until it's browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots, zucchini, red bell pepper, and fermented vegetables to the pot. Stir to combine.
Add the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Stir in the clear honey and cook for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs like parsley or cilantro.
